rulings.Case Title: CONCORDE EQUITY 11, LLC VS. Q-BLACK, et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc And Motion For Or Der Tto Instruct Receiver As To Distribution Of Proceeds And Final Accounting
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, July 29, 2011, line 2, KEVIN SINGER'S Motion For OrDer to Instruct Receiver As To Distribution Of Proceeds And Final Accounting. The Court grants the Receiver's Motion and Motion for an Order in its entirety. The Court instructs the Receiver to distribute the proceeds from the sale of the liquor license as follows: Distribution of funds in escrow to Kevin Singer, Superior Court Receiver is the amount of $17,295.55 and to Plaintiff Concorde Equity II in the amount of $58,635.05. There is to be no distribution of funds in escrow to CTA, DBI Beverage, Matagrano, Inc., Tony Carraci, Robin Reichert and Muse, Inc. The Court approved and Settles the Receiver's Final Report and Accounting. The Court further finds that the Receiver's actions were correct and proper and in the best interests of the Receivership. All bonds posted herein are exonerated. The Court retains jurisdiction over any matters or claims which may later arise concerning this Receivership estate. The Receiver is hereby discharged and the Receivership terminated. This case is dismissed without prejudice. =(302/LMG)

rulings.Case Title: RENE J. ZUNIGA et al VS. DEUTSCHE BANK NATIONAL TRUST COMPANY et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, July 29, 2011, line 6, DEFENDANT DEUTSCHE BANK NATIONAL TRUST COMPANY'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Defendant¿s Demurrer to the 1st cause of action (to set aside foreclosure) is sustained with leave to amend. Tender must be pled by a mortgager who seeks to set aside a voidable sale, as opposed to a void sale, where no tender must be pled. Therefore, Plaintiff must plead facts to show that the sale would be void, as opposed to voidable. Defendant¿s Demurrer to the 2nd cause of action (Violation of CC§2923.5) is sustained without leave to amend. §2923.5 affords no relief where a foreclosure sale has already occurred because the only remedy provided is postponement of the foreclosure sale. Defendant¿s Demurrer to the 3rd cause of action (Violation of CC§1632) is sustained without leave to amend. The Court already ruled at the OSC hearing that a mortgage is not subject to a translation requirement under §1632 unless for a particular type, such as a reverse mortgage. Plaintiff has failed to show that the mortgage here was of that particular type. Moreover, § 1632 only applies to real estate brokers, not to lenders or subsequent loan servicers. Defendant¿s Demurrer to the 4th cause of action (Violation of TILA) is sustained without leave to amend. This claim rests on alleged wrongdoing at the time of origination of the loan. This defendant was not involved in the loan origination. Defendant¿s Demurrer to the 5th cause of action is sustained with leave to amend. Plaintiff must allege wrongdoing by this Defendant not arising out of origination of the loan, of which this Defendant played no part. Plaintiff must also plead facts or circumstances to toll the 4-year Statute of Limitations or wrongdoing that occurred within the 4 years of when this case was filed. Defendant¿s Demurrer to the 6th and 7th causes of action is sustained with leave to amend. Plaintiff must plead facts to show that the discovery rule applies to toll the

rulings.Case Title: JOANNE REINHART VS. BANK OF AMERICA N.A., A DELAWARE CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Osc Re Preliminary Injunction And Tro.
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, July 29, 2011, line 7. Order to show cause re Preliminary Injunction And T.R.O. Plaintiff's request for a preliminary injunction is denied. First California Courts have rejected the plaintiff's holder in due course argument. Ferguson v. Anelo Mortgage, LLC (2011) 195 Cal.App. 4th 1618. Second, plaintiff's evidentiary showing is weak. Finally, plaintiff's argument that the mortgage has been paid is disingenuous. What plaintiff is really saying is that the investors in the P.S.A. recovered what they were owed under the scheme through mortgage insurance, not because plaintiff paid off the mortgage. =(302/LMG)
